nursing-adn.applicationWindow: Apply Oct 15 for Jan cohort; Feb 15 for Aug cohort (Accelerated ADN: Feb 15 for May cohort)
nextSteps: Attend a Health Sciences information session
Complete prerequisite courses (BIO-168, ENG-111) with a C or better
Obtain Nurse Aide I certification and current NC Nurse Aide Registry listing
Take the ATI TEAS entrance exam (Advanced or higher, or Total ≥ 74)
Submit the competitive nursing program application by the deadline
nursing-adn.notes: Points-based competitive ranking using TEAS scores and academic record. Background check, FBI fingerprinting, physical exam, and drug screen required after acceptance. CPR certification required.

practical-nursing.applicationWindow: Apply Oct 15 for Jan cohort; Feb 15 for Aug cohort
nextSteps: Complete BIO-168 and ENG-111 with a C or better
Obtain Nurse Aide I certification and current NC registry listing
Take the ATI TEAS exam (minimum score 65)
Meet with a Health Sciences advisor (healthsciences@cpcc.edu)
Submit transcripts and practical nursing application by the deadline
practical-nursing.notes: Requirements-based selective entry; all coursework must be completed with a C or better. Background check, physical exam, and drug screen required.

nurse-aide.applicationWindow: Open enrollment — sections available most semesters
nextSteps: Apply to Central Piedmont
Submit official transcripts showing high school graduation
Complete placement testing if needed to establish ENG-111 eligibility
Meet with an academic advisor
Register for NAS-101
nurse-aide.notes: Open admission; must be 18 by the start of clinical experiences. Background check, immunizations, and drug screen required at first class meeting. Completing the certificate satisfies the Nurse Aide I prerequisite for ADN/PN programs.

physical-therapist-assistant.applicationWindow: Apply by Mar 15 for Aug cohort
nextSteps: Complete prerequisite courses (BIO-168, ENG-111, MAT-110) with a C or better
Take the ATI TEAS exam
Submit official transcripts and the Physical Therapist Assistant program application by Mar 15
Complete physical exam including drug screen
Obtain CPR/First Aid certification before clinical courses
physical-therapist-assistant.notes: Points-based competitive selection using TEAS scores and academic history. Background check required for clinical placement. Program chair: 704-330-6284.

occupational-therapy-assistant.applicationWindow: Apply by Oct 15 for Jan cohort
nextSteps: Complete prerequisite general education courses with a C or better
Take the ATI TEAS exam
Submit Central Piedmont application and official transcripts
Submit OTA program application by Oct 15
Complete physical exam and drug screen after acceptance
occupational-therapy-assistant.notes: Competitive points-based selection. Program starts in January. All OTA prefix courses must be passed with C or better each semester to progress.

surgical-technology.applicationWindow: Apply by Mar 31 for Aug cohort
nextSteps: Apply to Central Piedmont and complete placement tests
Contact the Therapy and Acute Care Division for an admission packet (704-330-2722)
Submit the Surgical Technology program application by Mar 31
Complete physical exam, immunizations, and 13-panel drug screen
Obtain current CPR/AED certification
surgical-technology.notes: Competitive admission requiring a separate program application. Background check and US citizenship/residency verification required. Health and accident insurance required.

respiratory-therapy.applicationWindow: Apply by Mar 15 for Aug cohort
nextSteps: Apply to Central Piedmont
Obtain the Respiratory Therapy admissions packet from the program website
Submit the program-specific application by Mar 15
Complete physical exam and annual 13-panel drug screen
Provide proof of US citizenship or legal residency
respiratory-therapy.notes: Competitive admission; students must be selected to enter. Contact program office: 704-330-6795. All curriculum courses require a C or better to progress.

cardiovascular-technology.applicationWindow: Apply by Mar 15 for Aug cohort
nextSteps: Apply to Central Piedmont and complete placement tests
Visit the CVT program website for the admissions packet
Submit the Cardiovascular Technology program application by Mar 15
Complete physical exam, immunizations (tetanus, hepatitis B), TB test, and drug screen
Obtain current CPR certification
cardiovascular-technology.notes: Competitive admission; students must be selected to enter. Program Chair: 704-330-3045. Background check required for clinical placements. Health and accident insurance required.

polysomnography.applicationWindow: Apply in spring for Aug cohort (exact deadline: contact program chair 704-330-2722 ext. 3245)
nextSteps: Apply to Central Piedmont and complete placement tests
Complete BIO-163 and MED-121 with a C or better
Submit the Polysomnography admissions packet
Provide evidence of good physical and mental health with a physical examination
Submit official high school and college transcripts
polysomnography.notes: Points-based competitive selection using academic performance and field-related experience. All PSG prefix courses require a C or better each semester to progress. Exact application deadline not published online — confirm with program chair.

medical-laboratory-technology.applicationWindow: Apply by Apr 1 for Aug cohort
nextSteps: Complete BIO-163, CHM-130/130A, and college-level English with a C or better
Take the TEAS exam and include scores in the admissions packet (waived for BS holders)
Submit the MLT admissions packet by Apr 1
Provide evidence of good physical and mental health
Complete criminal background check as required for clinical placement
medical-laboratory-technology.notes: Selective admission; students must be chosen to enter. BS-degree holders are exempt from the TEAS requirement. Program Chair: 704-330-2722 ext. 3425.

pharmacy-technology.applicationWindow: Apply by May 1 for Aug cohort
nextSteps: Apply to Central Piedmont and complete placement tests
Demonstrate computer competency through coursework or testing
Submit official transcripts and pharmacy technology program application by May 1
Complete physical exam and 13-panel drug screen
Provide criminal background check results
pharmacy-technology.notes: Best-prepared competitive selection using standardized test scores, academic history, and field-related experience. Program Chair: 704-330-6432. Health and accident insurance required.

dental-hygiene.applicationWindow: Apply by Mar 15 for Aug cohort
nextSteps: Complete BIO-168, BIO-169, and CHM-121/121A with a C or better
Take the ATI TEAS exam
Submit official transcripts and the Dental Hygiene program application by Mar 15
Obtain current Health Care Provider CPR certification
Complete criminal background check as required
dental-hygiene.notes: Points-based competitive selection using TEAS scores, academic performance, and field experience. One of the most competitive CPCC Health Sciences programs. Immunizations and health documentation required.

dental-assisting.applicationWindow: Apply by Apr 30 for Aug cohort
nextSteps: Attend a virtual information session (required to apply)
Apply to Central Piedmont and submit official transcripts
Submit the Dental Assisting program application by Apr 30
Obtain current Health Care Provider CPR certification
Complete physical exam, blood and drug screen, and updated immunizations
dental-assisting.notes: Attendance at a virtual information session is required before applying. All DEN prefix courses must be completed with a C or better. Criminal background check may be required for clinical rotations.

ophthalmic-medical.applicationWindow: Apply by Dec 1 for Jan cohort; Jun 15 for Aug cohort
nextSteps: Apply to Central Piedmont and complete placement tests
Meet with an academic advisor
Submit Central Piedmont application and official transcripts
Obtain Health Care Provider CPR certification (required before clinical courses)
Complete criminal background check as required for clinical placement
ophthalmic-medical.notes: Selective entry with two start dates per year (January and August). Program Chair: 704-330-2722 ext. 3444. US citizenship or legal residency verification may be required.

emergency-medical-science.applicationWindow: Open enrollment — start most semesters (EMT certificate: must qualify for ENG-111 and be 17+ by last day of EMS-110)
nextSteps: Apply to Central Piedmont
Complete placement tests for English and math
Meet with an academic advisor and attend program orientation
Register for EMS-110 (EMT) to begin the certificate pathway
Contact ems@cpcc.edu for program advising
emergency-medical-science.notes: Degree track is open admission. The EMT certificate (C45340-C1) requires ENG-111 eligibility and minimum age 17 by last class day of EMS-110. Completing the degree track requires BIO-168 and general education coursework.

medical-assisting.applicationWindow: Apply by Oct 15 for Jan cohort; Mar 15 for Aug cohort
nextSteps: Apply to Central Piedmont and complete placement tests
Complete recommended prerequisite courses (biology, math, keyboarding)
Submit official transcripts and Medical Assisting program application by the deadline
Obtain CPR certification before MED-260 Clinical Practicum
Complete physical exam and drug screen; criminal background check may be required
medical-assisting.notes: Students are selected to enter; two cohort starts per year (January and August). ACA-122 College Transfer Success is highly recommended. All coursework requires a C or better to progress. Contact: 704-330-6482.
